rulings.Case Title: INTERSTATE FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CLEVAND WRECKING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 4, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFF INTERSTATE FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. UNDER LINDEN PARTNERS V. WILSHIRE LINDEN ASSOC., (1998) 62 CAL.APP.4TH 508, 522, THE COURT CAN SUMMARY ADJUDICATE WHETHER OR NOT DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO DEFEND. THE LANGUAGE OF THE SUBCONTRACT AND THE FRISBY AMENDED COMPLAINT (CONSIDERED BY THIS COURT NOT FOR THE TRUTH OF ITS ALLEGATIONS BUT TO SHOW WHETHER ALLEGATIONS TRIGGERED COVERAGE), PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY EVIDENCED THAT THE INDEMNITY PROVISION WAS TRIGGERED AND THE DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO DEFEND PLAINTIFF. ACCORDINGLY, THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO DEFENDANT TO SHOW THAT THE HARM TO FRISBY WAS SOLELY CAUSED BY WEBCOR. IT FAILED TO DO SO. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE TO CONDUCT FURTHER DISCOVERY IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MAKE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING AS TO WHAT IT MAY SPECIFICALLY UNDERCOVER THAT WOULD RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH ALVARDO VS. JOSEPH MCGOVERN et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 4, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH ALVARDO'S COUNSEL'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD IS GRANTED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LISA DAMMAN VS. IRA P. MCEVOY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 4, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT K. IRA MCEVOY, DEBRA WETHERBY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. AS TO DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENT THAT PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS BARRED BY THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION LAWS, DEFENDANT HAS SUBMITTED EVIDENCE TENDING TO SHOW PLAINTIFF WAS MORE LIKELY AN EMPLOYEE AND SUCCESSFULLY SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO PLAINTIFF. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTING CONFLICTING EVIDENCE SUFFICIENT TO RAISE TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING APPLICATION OF THE BORELLO FACTORS AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS AN EMPLOYEE OR INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. AS TO THE "VETERINARIAN RULE" DEFENSE, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING THE EXCEPTION TO THE "VETERINARIAN RULE" THAT DEFENDANT WAS AWARE OF SCOOBY'S VICIOUS PROPENSITIES. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC VS. VERSAILLES DEVELOPMENT CO., LLC, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 4, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT WILLIAM BANER'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FURTHER FACTS TO SUPPORT HIS CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST FILE THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS NEFF VS. POPE & POPE PROPERTIES, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 4, 2011, line 5, PLAINTIFF LOUIS NEFF'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. WHILE DEFENDANT IS CORRECT THAT REFORMATION CAUSE OF ACTION MAY BE TIME BARRED AND THE PLAINTIFF'S FRAUD ALLEGATION IS TENUOUS, THE BETTER PRACTICE IS TO ALLOW THE AMENDMENT AND PERMIT DEFENDANT TO TEST THE CAUSE OF ACTION BY DEMURRER. CAL. CASUALTY GENERAL INS. V. SUP. CRT. (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D 274. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. DANNER III et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 4, 2011, line 10, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO PLAINTIFF STEPHEN ENGLER IS GRANTED. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF WAS 39 YEARS OLD AT ALL TIMES THAT THE PROMOTIONAL EXAMINATION WAS ADMINISTERED AND GAVE RISE TO THE ELIGIBILITY LIST FROM WHICH PROMOTIONS WERE MADE. ACCORDINGLY PLAINTIFF CANNOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HIS CLAIMS OF AGE DISCRIMINATION OR FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION. FURTHERMORE, BECAUSE OF HIS INELIGIBILITY FOR PROMOTION DUE TO HIS FAILURE ON THE MAY 2008 PORTION OF THE EXAM, PLAINTIF'S DISCRIMINATION CLAIM ALSO FAILS. FINALLY, THE CONTINUING VIOLATION DOCTRINE DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF WAS NOT A MEMBER OF THE PROTECTED CLASS AT THE TIME OF THE INITIAL ALLEGED UNLAWFUL CONDUCT (THE EXAM). =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PARADISE ON 25TH CONDOMINIUMS ASSOCIATION VS. BRIAN L. MARTINEZ, AN UNMARRIED MAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Civil Assesment Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 4, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANT BRIAN L. MARTINEZ, AN UNMARRIED MAN Motion To Vacate Civil Assesment Judgment. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE CIVIL ASSESSMENT IS DENIED. THE FEES AND ASSESSMENTS ACCRUED AFTER DEFENDANT FILED FOR BANKRUPTCY. ACCORDINGLY, THE ACTIONS REGARDING THESE FEES AND ASSESSMENTS WERE NOT SUBJECT TO THE STAY UNDER THE BANKRUPTCY CODE. SEE 11 U.S.C.A. SECTION 362(a)(1). =(302/LMG)
